observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:
Examination and verification
a)Municipal Accountant (MA) to: (i) conduct a thorough examination and verification of its books to identify all reconciling items, including errors that were not recorded, and evaluate/determine the causes of discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustments to establish the correct balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA at year-end; (ii) prepare SL for all bank accounts and ensure these SL are updated regularly; and (iii) furnish copy of the SL and general ledgers (GL) to the Auditor’s Office; b) AMT to conduct thorough examination and verification of its books to identify and record all outstanding reconciling items including errors that were not taken up in the books; and c) MAO and the MTO to adjust immediately their records to bring their Cash in Bank records into agreement.
We recommended the Management to require the:
(a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) regularly notify the payees of checks that have remained unclaimed/unreleased; and (ii) initiate inquiries with the respective payees regarding the status of stale checks to determine whether there is a need to replace the previously issued checks or consider their cancellation, based on the procedures enumerated in Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs; and b) MA to (i) effect adjusting entries to restore the total amount of unclaimed/unreleased checks as of year-end in the Cash in Bank – LCCA account and recognize the appropriate liability account to reflect the correct balances of these accounts at year-end; (ii) exert utmost effort in obtaining the supporting documents necessary for the reconciling items; and (iii) conduct identification and verification of all outstanding reconciling items that have not yet been recorded in the books and thereafter furnish the Auditor’s Office with copies of the JEVs, for verification purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to instruct the MA to (a) coordinate with the Provincial Accounting Office to address the discrepancies in the reciprocal accounts, Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs, and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the accounts; (b) maintain SL of the Due to LGUs and Due from LGUs account to facilitate the reconciliation; (c) conduct verification of the Due from LGUs account to establish the entity for which the Municipality has valid claims, assess its collectability and recognize impairment loss, if any; and (d) communicate with the source LGUs and perform reconciliation to ascertain the nature of the transferred funds, whether the funds received require liquidation or are to be treated as subsidy to the LGU; otherwise, prepare and submit liquidation reports for the expended portion and cause the return to the source agencies of all unutilized balances of the fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: The MA was amenable with the above observations and stated that they are already preparing the GLs and SLs of the Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs account. He explained that the discrepancy with the Provincial Accounting Office relates to the transfer of RPTs. Additionally, he noted that they have communicated with the Provincial Accounting Office and that the reconciliation of their records is ongoing, as they are still retrieving some supporting documents, including liquidation reports.

observations.recommendation:

a) instruct the MA to prepare the adjusting entries to: (i) recognize the ₱413,460.53 disallowances which had become final and executory in the books of the Municipality; (ii) reverse the original entry for the settlement of the receivables; and (iii) effectuate the following proposed adjusting entry to correct the Receivables-Disallowances/ Charges account:
Due from Officers and Employees xx
Other Receivables xx
Cash-LCCA xx

b) require the MA and AMT to (i) take the necessary actions to expedite the collection of the unsettled disallowances; (ii) exhaust all possible remedies to collect from each of the liable persons; c) subsequently, cause all the certifying and approving officers, who took part in the disbursement of disallowed expenditures, to assume responsibility in the final settlement of the remaining balance of the disallowance; and d) ensure that before allowing settlement of disallowances through installment payment the same was authorized and approved by COA.
observations.managementAction: The MA stated that he is amenable with the audit findings and assured compliance thereof before the end of the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management instruct the:
(a) MA to prepare and submit to the Audit Team, the subsidiary ledger for their Due to GSIS account; and
(b) AMT to (i) remit in full all GSIS contributions or payments withheld from accounts of employees on or before the prescribed due dates in accordance with Sections 14 and 15 of the IRR of RA No. 8291; and (ii) strictly monitor the remittance of withheld amounts to prevent accumulation and incurrence of unnecessary penalties.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, the MA stated that the discrepancies were caused by timing differences. Nevertheless, he assured that they will conduct reconciliation with the GSIS Lucena Branch to ensure that all outstanding contributions are promptly remitted.

a) stop the grant of additional TA to the AMT; and b) require the concerned official to refund the claim of additional TA amounting to ₱78,000.00 due to violation with Section 9.0 of DBM LBC No. 103.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:
a) instruct the AMT to (i) keep the custody of accountable forms to its respective offices; (ii) restrict access to areas where accountable forms and other sensitive documents are kept; (iii) establish strict protocols for visitors and unauthorized individuals entering the treasurer's office; (iv) ensure the MTO are equipped with secured storage units; and (v) properly monitor the accountable forms and prepare the monthly CRAAF and RAAF correctly and promptly submit the same to COA; and b) conduct proper investigation on the aforementioned incident and determine whether the AMT should be charged with negligence for failure to use reasonable care of these receipts.
observations.managementAction:
The Office of the Mayor will investigate the incident and inform the auditor the result of such investigation. Actions planned and discussed: keep custody of accountable forms to its respective offices; restrict access to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office only to its authorized personnel; establish strict protocols for visitors entering the MTO; install CCTV cameras within MTO to monitor movement of personnel and processing of documents; ensure all storage are secured; properly monitor accountable forms and prepare monthly CRAAF and RAAF and submit to COA.
Status: Not Implemented — actions to be implemented; investigation ongoing.
observations.reason: The investigation is on-going; no action taken yet to charge or sanction; implementation of controls pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert utmost effort in updating the RPT Account Registry consistent with the assessed value of the properties from the Municipal Assessor’s Office and provide the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with the accurate list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; b) require the Municipal Accountant to draw the JEV to set-up RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of the year based on the duly certified list provided by the Municipal Treasurer; and c) direct both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the difference noted between the: (i) the recorded amount of RPT and SET receivables in the municipality’s books and the records of Real Property Delinquencies maintained by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office; and (ii) the amount credited to the RPT and SET receivables and the amount collected based on the Summary of RPT provided by MTO.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Assessor will expedite the computerized assessment and provide database integrated with systems.
Municipal Treasurer to exert utmost effort in updating RPT registry and provide accurate list to MA.
Municipal Accountant to draw JEV to set-up receivables and reconcile with Treasurer's records. Additional JO personnel requested to assist retrieval and reconciliation. Status: Not Implemented — actions ongoing and pending staffing and system improvements.
observations.reason: Vacancy of positions and lack of computerized system; insufficient manpower to reconcile and prepare subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to (a) compute the correct depreciation expense of the PPEs taking into consideration the factors in determining the useful life of an asset as provided in Paragraph 72 of IPSAS No. 17 and record immediately in the books of accounts to fairly present the carrying value of the Property Accounts in the FS; (b) prepare the necessary journal entries to record adjustments in the government equity account for prior year’s unrecorded depreciation; and (c) maintain ledger cards for each category of Property items and prepare lapsing schedule to render correct and accurate valuation of the Property Accounts and in due course facilitate the fair presentation in the FS.
observations.managementAction: No adjusting journal entries were made in the books of account. The Municipal Accountant assured compliance. Status: Not Implemented — no entries recorded.
observations.reason: No action taken; lack of preparation of adjusting JEVs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:
a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to: (i) refrain from directly recording the purchase of inventories for distribution and financial assistance to NGOs/POs to the Donation account and comply with the guidelines provided in Section 114, Chapter 7 of MNGAS for LGUs, Paragraph 44 of IPSAS No. 12 and COA Circular No. 2015-009; (ii) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the recording of financial assistance to NGOs/POs; and (iii) ensure that expenditures are supported with complete documentation; b) require the officials in-charge of the distribution of the inventories to immediately submit the actual distribution lists per barangay showing the names, address, signature of recipient, actual inventories received and actual date of receipt to facilitate conduct of validation; and c) submit Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the NGOs/POs to support the payments; and d) stop granting subsidy to NGOs/POs that do not clearly define the projects as enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.
observations.managementAction: The Management has yet to conduct any action with regard to the recommendation. No updates have been provided as of date.
observations.reason: Failure of the officers-in-charge to submit the necessary distribution lists and supporting documents; lack of MOAs and liquidation reports.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the:
a) Municipal Treasurer continuously conduct extensive collection of water service fees from delinquent water subscribers to augment the income of the Municipality and devise strategies to make these subscribers aware on the regulations of the Municipality particularly in the payment of their obligations; and
b) Municipal Administrator (i) ensure that the present water pumps are in good operating conditions to be able to provide adequate water supply for the subscribers and that all subscribers are installed with functional water meters; and (ii) take immediate actions on requests for repairs, conduct periodic inspection and calibration of water meters and replace defective ones to ensure the reliability and accuracy of consumption of every household.
observations.managementAction: No significant increase in collection of delinquent water subscribers. No action plan provided. The Municipal Administrator to undertake repairs and meter replacements; Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection. Status: Not Implemented.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower; vacancy in key positions; some subscribers ignore demand to pay the water bills on time.

observations.managementAction: The Management has yet to conduct any action with regard to the recommendation. No updates have been provided as of date.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Management: a) prioritize the appointment of vacant permanent positions by hiring qualified personnel and expedite the promotion of deserving employees to comply with the provisions of RA No. 7160 as well as to provide equal job opportunities to the community; b) require the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to publish the Notice of Vacancy and to fast track the selection of qualified applicants for the six vacant plantilla positions and the subsequent appointment thereof; and c) consider to evaluate the performance of individuals under Job Order Contracts to open opportunity for possible appointment to permanent/regular positions.
observations.managementAction: The Management has yet to conduct any action with regard to the recommendation. No updates have been provided as of date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to a) submit the (i) year-end FS and the Post-Closing Trial Balance every February 14 after the end of the calendar year; (ii) Pre-closing Trial Balance and Subsidiary Schedule of General Ledger at every twentieth after the end of the month; and (iii) complete Interim Financial Statements at the end of every quarter lest their salaries be suspended until the submission of the requirements of the Commission has been complied with; and b) be mindful of the deadlines and adhere strictly with the provisions of Sections 70, 72, 73 of MNGAS, Volume I, and Section 41(2) of PD No. 1445, and Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 on the submission of trial balance, financial reports and year-end financial statements and supporting schedules of LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The MAO has failed to submit the FS and trial balances within the prescribed deadline. Not Implemented. The MAO has failed to submit the FS and trial balances within the prescribed deadline. No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to: c) prepare necessary adjusting entries to exclude the amount of penalties recorded as part of RPT and SET receivables; and d) exclude penalties in the RPT and SET receivables account and comply with Sections 20 and 23 Chapter 3 Volume 1 MNGAS for LGUs, the definition of taxes in the IPSAS 23, and Circular no. 2015-009 in setting up the amount of RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of the year, to facilitate the fair presentation of the RPT and SET receivables in the financial statements.

observations.managementAction: No verification and review of the books was made by management for their Due from LGU account. Not Implemented No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require: a) the Municipal Accountant to (i) verify the account used on the above-listed on-going projects and make necessary adjustments of the affected PPE accounts as a result of misclassification; (ii) draw appropriate journal entries to recognize the Construction Materials Inventory upon purchase of construction materials; (iii) recognize the Construction in Progress account upon issuance and utilization of construction materials as well as upon receipt of progress billings from the contractors regardless of the source of funds; and (iv) ensure that depreciation of asset begins when it is available for use pursuant to the provisions of IPSAS and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; b) the Municipal Engineer to provide promptly the MAO with copies of the report of completed projects and other necessary documents; and c) the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to reconcile their records regularly to determine the status and correct balances of the affected CIP and PPE accounts.

Adjust the accounts balances accordingly. 4. Inform the auditor of the adjustment made and provide copies of documents that will substantiate the adjustments and balances reported.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and the Management agreed to: a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) observe strictly the updated description of accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009; (ii) reclassify the expenses to proper inventory or expense account; and (iii) furnish copy of RCALGU to all offices/end-users for proper identification of accounts in the PPMP to be submitted to the Municipal Budget Office; and b) require the Municipal Budget Office to adopt the RCALGU as prescribed under COA Circular No. 20215-009 in the preparation of the agency’s budget.
observations.managementAction: There were still misclassified accounts noted in the books. Not Implemented No action taken. No adjusting entries were made. The Municipal Budget Office being used in recording the annual budget are under review; ensure that the annual investment plans and appropriations are prepared using proper account code and title.
observations.reason: No action taken; accounting staff did not reclassify accounts; budget office yet to adopt RCALGU.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a) strictly enforce settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2020; b) remind the officers and employees to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period; and c) issue demand letters to enforce/require the immediate settlement and/or refund of the amount advanced, and enforce the withholding of salaries of those who fail to settle/liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period after the demand.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Management to: a) direct the drivers of the service vehicles to properly accomplish the Daily Trip Tickets and attach the same to the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels regularly; b) require the Municipal Administrator to install control measures on the disbursements of fuel to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption and ensure strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 for the proper accounting and control of fuel consumption of government vehicles and other machineries and equipment of the Municipality; and c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure proper processing of transactions by religiously requiring complete documentation before payment.
observations.managementAction: The Management has yet to conduct any action with regard to the recommendation. Not Implemented; drivers to be instructed to properly accomplish Daily Trip Tickets, install control measures, and ensure proper processing of transactions.
observations.reason: Some drivers failed to submit Daily Trip Tickets and Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption; lack of controls and enforcement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to refrain from allowing individuals hired under Job Order contracts to attend seminars, meetings and other activities which requires claiming of travelling expense. Otherwise, stipulate a condition in the contract or hire them as permanent employee in order for them to legally claim reimbursements of expenses incurred during their authorized travel.
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Management: b) instruct the Human Resource and Management Officer to: i. evaluate the current regular personnel plantilla to determine if there are existing positions similar to the designations given to JOs; and ii. in case none, make representation with the higher management to create positions to help augment the current workforce of those departments in need; and c) in case none, make representation with the higher management to create positions to help augment the current workforce.
observations.managementAction: The Management has yet to conduct any action with regard to the recommendation. Not Implemented — no actions taken.
observations.reason: No action taken; vacancies and personnel issues remain unresolved.

Office;

receivables.

infrastructure projects by monitoring the performance of the

coordinate with the Provincial Accounting Office to address the

LGUs, and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct
balances of the accounts; (b) maintain SL of the Due to LGUs and Due
from LGUs account to facilitate the reconciliation; (c) conduct

b) Non-adjustment of reconciling items, stale checks and unreleased checks in the books
Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs provides that a check is stale, if it has been outstanding for over six months from date of issue or as prescribed by the depository bank while check is considered spoil when, it is torn, mutilated, defaced or with erasures/errors affecting the genuineness of any material information contained therein, said checks may be cancelled when they become spoiled or stale. The manual also provides that at least one month before a check becomes stale, the Treasurer shall send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check. A spoiled or stale check shall be marked cancelled on its face and reported following the procedures enumerated in the said section. While Sections 42 and 43 of the same manual, provides that the Municipal Treasurer shall maintain a Check Register where all checks issued shall be recorded chronologically and where the claimants shall be required to acknowledge receipt thereof and that the checks released to claimants shall be reported in the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) which shall be prepared daily by the Treasurer for each fund.  It shall be submitted to the Accountant for preparation of Journal Entry Voucher based on individual checks issued and recording in the Check Disbursements Journal.
Item 3 of Commission on Audit (COA) No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 states that the depository/servicing banks shall furnish the Local Accountant with the Bank Statements including debit/credit memos, paid checks, etc., within five (5) days after the end of each month which shall be the basis for the preparation of the monthly BRS. The Local Accountants shall within ten (10) days from receipt of the Bank Statements, reconcile the bank statement with the General Ledgers and prepare the BRS and shall draw journal vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the GL.
Paragraph 7 of IPSAS No. 1 states that Accrual basis of accounting under which transactions and other events are recognized when they occur (and not only when cash or its equivalent is received or paid). Therefore, the transactions and events are recorded in the accounting records and recognized in the financial statements of the period to which they relate. In the said paragraph defined Assets as resources controlled by an entity as a result of past events and from which future economic benefits or service potential are expected to flow to the entity.
For Calendar Year 2021, the Municipality maintained three current accounts with the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) – Boac Branch for all its funds with the corresponding bank balances as of December 31, 2021, listed as follows:
Review of the BRS of the three bank accounts maintained with the LBP as of December 31, 2021, disclosed that there were 155 outstanding checks with aggregate amount of ₽10,341,880.55, 63 checks of which were already staled. Summary is presented below:
Based on the table presented, the 63 checks were carried out in the BRS as outstanding checks for over 6 months and more without effecting the necessary cancellations or adjustments to restore in the cash in bank account and recognize the corresponding payable at the end of the year. This has been raised in the previous year’s Audit Report but remained unaddressed until this year. As these checks have not yet been presented by payees to the banks for the past years, existence of actual claims or enforcement of payments to the payees may be considered now as doubtful.
As per report of unclaimed/unreleased checks by the Municipal Treasurer showed that there were no unreleased and unclaimed checks as of year-end. However, verification of the submitted vouchers particularly for the month of December revealed that there were checks issued only to payees in CY 2022. Further review of the BRS disclosed that out of the 155 checks totalling ₽10,341,880.55 reported outstanding as of December 31, 2021,     35 checks amounting to ₽7,437,531.86 remained unclaimed and unreleased to payees, details as follows:
Outstanding checks refers to those checks which were already issued to payee but has not been encashed or presented to the bank. The above-listed unreleased/unclaimed checks at year-end were treated as outstanding checks in the BRS when it should have been reverted/restored to the Cash in Bank account as these were still in the possession of the Municipality at year-end and the appropriate liability account should likewise be recognized at year-end. The non-preparation of the RCI required under Section 43 of the MNGAS brought difficulty in monitoring the issuance of checks for all funds and have no clear cut-off as to the period for which these checks relate to.
Aside from the stale checks and unreleased/unclaimed checks, there were reconciling items totalling ₽106,726.26 which remained unadjusted in the books, to wit:
The above reconciling items were also raised in previous years’ audit, these includes erroneous deposits from one fund to another which remained unadjusted in the books dates as far as May 20, 2005. There were also erroneous deposits totalling ₽12,207.24 which were made to a PNB closed account of the Municipality that need to be adjusted. Though these were considered in the preparation of BRS, the MAO have not prepared Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to effect adjustment of these long outstanding reconciling items in the books, hence defeats the purpose for which the reconciliation statements were made.
Inquiry with the MAO revealed that the above listed checks were recognized as outstanding checks as of year-end. She explained that in the preparation of BRS, outstanding checks were recognized by comparing all the checks issued by the Municipality with the bank statement issued by the bank. Any checks not cleared by the bank were immediately recognized as outstanding checks regardless whether these were actually received by payees or had became stale in the hands of the payees. As to checks which were still in the possession of the Municipal Treasurer at year-end, she admitted that the necessary adjusting entries were not effected as the Office was not furnished with the list of unclaimed/unreleased claims as of year-end. The Municipal Accountant was amenable on the existence of stale checks and committed to prioritize the preparation of JEV to effect the cancellation in the books while those long outstanding reconciling entries, he admitted that he was having difficulties in tracing when and how these errors occurred, nonetheless he also committed to effect the corresponding adjusting entries.
The inability to prepare and effect necessary adjusting entries for the above stale, unclaimed/unreleased checks and reconciling items resulted to understatement of the balances of Cash in Bank - LCCA by ₽8,519,832.51, appropriate Liabilities by ₽8,507,625.27 and understated the Government Equity account by ₽12,207.24 at year-end.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
prepare and maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for the accounts receivable, consequently, prepare an aging schedule in accordance with Sec. 111 and 114 of PD No. 1445 and Section 4 (e) Chapter 2, Volume 1, of the MNGAS for LGUs to monitor the collectability of the Accounts Receivable and be able to estimate and determine potential impairment losses to be recognized in the books to present fairly the Accounts Receivable in the financial statements,
prepare and maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for the accounts payable, and afterwhich prepare an aging schedule to establish the validity and existence of the accounts payable and determine dormant accounts.
revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund the accounts payable which has been in the books for more than 2 years and with unfounded claims, and
exert effort in locating documents that will provide information as to details of long outstanding payables and receivables.
Management Comment
The Municipal Accountant during exit conference informed the Audit Team that they started the preparation of the aging schedules for its Accounts Receivables only last year. He mentioned that bulk of the recorded receivables were from real property taxes and income from local economic enterprises of the Municipality and admitted that his office is having a hard time gathering data from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office as the required data were manually prepared. While on the Accounts Payable, he admitted that substantial amount of the recorded payables lacks supporting documents and lacks details to whom the Municipality owed to. He was amenable on the non-maintenance of complete SLs both for the Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable. Nevertheless, he assured the Audit Team of their compliance with the recommendations and made a commitment to continue the preparation of the required aging schedules.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to (i) coordinate with the Provincial Accounting Office to address the discrepancies in the reciprocal accounts, Due from LGUs and Due to LGU and record the shares of barangays directly in their respective books; (ii) effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct amounts of receivable, payable and income accounts in the books at year-end; (iii) make representation with the Provincial Government for the release/remittance of  receivables amounts due to the Municipality; and (iv) as to the amount in the beginning balance of the Due from LGU account of the Municipality, conduct verification and review of books to establish the entity for which the Municipality has valid claims and to identify any errors occurred in the book, once completed, furnished the Auditor’s Office of said schedule for validation purposes.
Management Comment
The Municipal Accountant during exit conference is amenable on the noted observations and admitted that he was not aware on those receivables of the municipality recorded in the books of the PGM. While for those amounts payable to the PGM, he explained that these represents the share of the province on the collections of real property taxes. He justified that the differences were caused by timing difference in the recording and remittance of said share. Nonetheless, the Municipal Accountant committed to personally communicate with the Provincial Accounting Office for the reconciliation of their records and once reconciled, to furnish the Audit Team of the JEV for the adjustments made in the books. He also assured the Audit Team of their compliance with the recommendations for the ensuing years.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to (a) prepare and maintain the required subsidiary ledgers to determine at any time the details and status of the intra-agency accounts; and (b) prioritize the reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts in order to identify possible errors/adjustments that need to be adjusted in the books
The Municipal Accountant is amenable on the noted observation and admitted that there were erroneous transfers made in their fund. He committed to prepare and maintain the required SL for all funds and verify the cause of the discrepancy between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds account in conformity with the above audit recommendations.

We recommended the Management to:
require both the Municipal Accountant and MT to maintain and update their respective Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, to facilitate reconciliation of accounting and property records;
the Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical count of inventories and submit complete and properly accomplished RPCI to the Audit Team and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation; and
consider to reorganize the composition of the Inventory Committee to make said committee functional.
The Municipal Treasurer admitted that her office was not maintaining the required stock cards for the Municipality’s inventory accounts. She added that the Municipal Inventory Committee were only conducting physical counts for all property plant and equipment. While the Municipal Accountant express his adherence to the noted observation.
Purchases of inventories totaling ₱9,583,713.85 were directly recorded as outright expense contrary to Section 114, Chapter 7 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I which prescribes the use of the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for inventories, resulting to the understatement of the inventory and overstatement of the related expense accounts in the financial statements as at December 31, 2021.
Section 114, Chapter 7 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I , states that:
Perpetual Inventory Method-Purchase of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, shall be recorded as inventory following the perpetual inventory method. Under the perpetual inventory method, an inventory control account is maintained in the General Ledger on a current basis. In addition, detailed inventory records are maintained for each inventory item.
Regular purchases shall be coursed thru the inventory account and issuances thereof shall be recorded as they take place, except those purchased out of the petty cash fund which shall be for immediate use and for stock in which case shall be charged immediately to the appropriate expense accounts
Review of the Check Disbursement Journal revealed that the Municipality directly charge purchases of supplies and materials totalling ₱9,583,713.85 to the related expense account, summarized below:
Verification of records showed that these purchases were directly recorded as outright expenses instead of following the Perpetual Inventory Method which requires that purchases must first be recorded to its specific inventory accounts and subsequently treated as an expense once the supplies and materials are actually consumed or issued to the end-user.
The recording of inventories directly as expenses resulted in the understatement of inventory and overstatement of expense accounts on the period the purchase was made until such time that the items were totally consumed or issued to the end-user.
The Municipal Accountant admitted that it is their practice to charge the supplies and materials to the expense account upon acquisition. Considering that this is a reiteration of previous years’ audit observation, the Municipal Accountant was still not able to adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method.
The erroneous recording of inventories resulted in inaccurate balances of inventory and expenses accounts in the financial statements corresponding to the value of remaining stocks at year-end which were directly recorded as expenses. Due to the erroneous practice of directly charging the inventories to the expense account, the need to maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) and Stock Cards (SCs) of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, respectively, and conduct of inventory count are necessary to verify the existence and correctness of the inventory accounts and determine the remaining inventories on stock as of December 31, 2021, if any.

We recommended that the Management:
require the Municipal Inventory Committee to make utmost effort to achieve a more in depth and complete, physical count of all PPEs properly classified by type/category and ensure that at least one of the members of the Inventory Committee possess technical knowledge to identify those assets which were not initially included in the count conducted; and
require both Municipal Accounting Office and MTO/GSO to prepare and maintain their respective records and ensure that these records are regularly reconciled so that discrepancies could be investigated promptly and adjustments, if necessary, will be effected accordingly.
Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) under the General Fund (GF) and Special Education Fund (SEF) with a book value of ₱4,567,318.77 were still not provided with the prescribed depreciation contrary to Paragraphs 71 and 72 of International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 17 thereby overstating the PPE and understating the Depreciation Expense account by the amount of the depreciation that should have been provided for the year.
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Management requires the Municipal Accountant to (a) compute the correct depreciation expense of the PPEs taking into consideration the factors in determining the useful life of an asset as provided in Paragraph 72 of IPSAS No. 17 and record immediately in the books of accounts to fairly present the carrying value of the PPES in the financial statements; (b) prepare necessary journal entries to record adjustments in the government equity account for prior year’s unrecorded depreciation; and (c) maintain ledger cards for each category of PPE items and prepare lapsing schedule to render accurate the valuation of the PPE and in due course present fairly the financial statements, pursuant to Sections 58, 111 and 114 of PD No. 1445.
The Municipal Treasurer justified to the Audit Team that they are exerting their utmost efforts but admitted the weakness of the Committee particularly the lack of technical knowledge of the members in identifying government assets.  She committed that once her records are updated, she will reconcile their records with the Municipal Accounting Office. Meanwhile, the Municipal Accounting Office stated that the results of the complete physical count to be conducted by the Municipal Inventory Committee will be his basis for the reconciliation of records.

We reiterated to the Management to a) create a Committee which will be responsible for the registration of all real estate owned by the Municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386; b) upon completion of the land titling, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary entries and record the cost incurred in the purchase or fair value if acquired through donation or transfers without cost of the land; and c) direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that the Municipality has absolute or legal ownership over the properties before implementing the construction of buildings and other structures.
Management Comment
The Municipal Assessor during exit conference was amenable on the observation and admitted that from the time this observation was raised they were having difficulty in creating a committee in charged for the registration of all real estate owned by the municipality. He added that he initiated the preparation of the required documents for titling however, during preparation and securing of documents, the Municipal Assessor encountered problems on how said parcels of land were actually acquired by the Municipality particularly those acquired through donation as the proof of transfer can no longer be retrieved nor in scripted. He concluded that the titling of said parcels of land could no longer be pursued.
The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant are both amenable that those properties where the infrastructure projects were implemented were not covered by titles.  They added that included in the lists of lands were properties where roads were constructed, thus the need for titling is no longer necessary.
Auditor’s Rejoinder
The Audit Team stressed the importance of titling of real properties under the name of the Municipality in order to support the assertion of ownership, existence and accuracy    of the Land account reflected in the financial statement as well as to protect the Municipality of the risk of losing government funds due to future legal actions or future claims of ownership by third parties, by the donors or their heirs.

We recommended that the Management require a) the Municipal Accountant to (i) verify the account used on the above-listed on-going projects and make necessary adjustments of the affected PPE accounts as a result of misclassification; (ii) draw appropriate journal entries to recognize the Construction Materials Inventory upon purchase of construction materials; (iii) recognize the Construction in Progress account upon issuance and utilization of construction materials as well as upon receipt of progress billings from the contractors regardless of the source of funds; and (iv) ensure that depreciation of asset begins when it is available for use pursuant to the provisions of IPSAS and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and b) the Municipal Engineer to provide promptly the MAO with copies of the report of completed projects and other necessary documents; and c) the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to reconcile their records regularly to determine the status and correct balances of the affected CIP and PPE accounts.
The Municipal Engineer committed to furnish the Municipal Accounting Office copy of the report on the status of project implementation at year-end in order to update and reconcile their records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:

a) instruct the designated LDRRM officer to properly plan and monitor the timely and full implementation of LDRRM Fund towards the achievement of the objectives of the Republic Act No. 10121 to ensure the Municipality’s preparedness in times of calamity;

b) require the MDRRM Council to include the total appropriations of the current and previous years’ appropriations in the LDRRM Plan in order to fully utilized the government funds towards addressing the risk reduction and management issues within the Municipality; and

c) require the Municipal Accountant to follow the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 relative to the reporting requirements of the DRRMF, specifically the proper transfer of the previous years’ unexpended balances of the DRRMF to the Trust Fund. Only the unexpended balances of DRRMF for 30% Quick Response Fund and MOOE of the 70% Mitigation Fund shall be transferred to the Trust Liability account.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer complied and submitted to the Audit Team  the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to: a) require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to take up the cancellation of the stale checks and revert them to cash as prescribed in GAAM Volume I; and b) reflect in the books the reconciling items found during reconciliation especially those that dates back to 2005 to reflect the correct balance of the cash accounts in their Financial Statements.

We reiterated and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to trace and record the adjustments in order to reconcile the differences between the recorded amount of RPT and SET receivables and the records of Real Property Delinquencies maintained by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor should maintain and regularly update the Real Property Tax Account Register/taxpayer’s index cards to facilitate the accurate computation and presentation of the RPT and SET receivables in the books.

Management Comment

The Municipal Accountant assured the Audit Team that the noted discrepancies in the balances of RPT and SET Receivables will be traced and adjusted accordingly in CY 2021 Books of Accounts.

The Audit Team recommended that the Management:

instruct the MTO to furnish the Municipal Accounting Office, on periodic basis, the necessary report/document pertinent to collectibles from market and cemetery rentals and operation of waterworks system for recording purposes; and
require the Municipal Accounting Office to draw the necessary journal entries in the books to record the receivables from unpaid market and cemetery rentals together with the uncollected water fees.

Management Comment

The Municipal Treasurer’s Office explained to the Audit Team that the Office submits reports pertaining to the collectibles from Waterworks, Market and Cemetery Operations to the Office of the Mayor/Municipal Administrator on quarterly basis. Nonetheless, she assured that the Municipal Accounting Office will be furnished of such report.

The Municipal Accountant justified that the recognition of accrued income for rentals and water service fees were not required when PPSAS were adopted and implemented in 2015. Hence no accrual was taken up in the books of accounts except for the Real Property Tax Receivables. But assured the Audit Team that accrual of revenues from the afore-mentioned operations will be taken up in the CY 2021 Books of Accounts.

The Audit Team recommended the Management to: a) require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory count of the supplies and materials and submit the RPCI to this office and furnish the same to the MAO for reconciliation; and b) require the Municipal Accountant to adopt Perpetual Inventory Method in recording inventories as prescribed under Section 490 of GAAM, Volume I to present fairly the actual inventory count in its Financial Statements.

Management Comment

The Inventory Committee committed to conduct physical inventory count of supplies and materials thru proper coordination with the concerned department heads, and will submit the Report on the Physical Count on Inventories (RCPI) to the Provincial Auditor’s Office and furnish the same the Municipal Accounting Office.
The Municipal Accountant stated that he will discuss the adoption of Perpetual Inventory Method in recording inventories to concerned offices. Procurement, custody and issuance/usage of supplies are presently managed by each department and will recommend the maintenance of stock cards for each supply in every department.

The Audit Team recommended that the Management:

require the Municipal Inventory Committee to make utmost effort to achieve a more in depth, if not complete, physical count of all PPEs; ensure that at least one of the members of the Inventory Committee possess technical knowledge to identify those assets which were not initially included in the count conducted, and ensure that properties were properly classified by type/category;
compel the MTO/GSO to prepare and maintain complete and updated Property Cards, per type/category on all PPE as recorded in the books of accounts; and
require both MTO/GSO and Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile property records with the accounting records and adjust the books of accounts and the property records, as warranted, after reconciliation.

Management Comment

The Members of the Municipal Inventory Committee justified to the Audit Team that they are exerting their utmost efforts but admitted the weakness of the Committee particularly the lack of technical knowledge of the members in identifying government assets. The MTO/GSO disclosed that the Office had purchased additional Property Cards and is maintaining complete and updated Property Cards per type or category per Audit Team’s recommendations. She committed that once her records are updated, she will reconcile their records with the Municipal Accounting Office. Meanwhile, the Municipal Accounting Office stated that the results of the complete physical count to be conducted by the Municipal Inventory Committee will be his basis for the reconciliation of records.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant (a) compute the correct depreciation expense of the PPEs taking into consideration the factors in determining the useful life of an asset as provided in Paragraph 72 of IPSAS No. 17 and record immediately in the books of accounts in order to fairly present the carrying value of the PPES in the financial statements; and (b) prepare necessary journal entries to record adjustments in the government equity account for prior year’s unrecorded depreciation.

We reiterated to the Management to (a) create a Committee which will be responsible for the registration of all real estate owned by the Municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386; (b) upon completion of the land titling, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary entries and record the cost incurred in the purchase or fair value if acquired through donation or transfers without cost of the land; and c) direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that the Municipality has absolute or legal ownership over the properties before implementing the construction of buildings and other structures.

Management Comment

The Management was amenable on the observation and admitted that they are having difficulty in creating a committee for the registration of all real estate owned by the municipality. Nevertheless, they committed to adhere on the offered recommendations. The Municipal Engineer likewise assured the Audit Team that all infrastructure projects will be implemented once all documents of legal ownership have been secured.

We reiterated that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to (i) draw appropriate journal entries to recognize the Construction Materials Inventory upon purchase of construction materials; (ii) recognize the Construction in Progress account upon issuance and utilization of construction materials; (iii) recognize PPE account only when it is probable that future economic benefits or service potential associated with the item will flow to the entity and the cost or fair value of the item can be measured reliably; and (iv) ensure that depreciation of asset begins when it is available for use pursuant to the provisions of IPSAS and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

Management Comment

The Municipal Accountant will review and compute the composition of Construction Materials Inventory and Construction in Progress accounts and the necessary adjusting journal entries will be recorded to effect the adjustments.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the Management agreed to:

instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) strictly observe/follow the updated description of accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009; (ii) reclassify the expenses to proper inventory or expense account; and (iii) furnish copy of Chart of Accounts to all offices/end-users for proper identification of accounts in the PPMP to be submitted to the Municipal Budget Office.
instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to adopt the new account codes as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 in the preparation of the agency’s budget.

Management Comment

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer committed the adoption of Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs in the preparation of the Annual Budget.

We reiterated that Management require the (a) Inventory Committee to expedite the conduct of complete the inventory taking and immediately submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Municipal Accounting Office; (b) Municipal Treasurer’s Office to coordinate with the Accounting Office for the proper classification of the PPE in order to reconcile with the accounting records; and (c) require the both Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards and property plant and equipment ledger cards, and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count and facilitate in the reconciliation of inventory reports and accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The Management partially conducted physical count of Property Plant and Equipment owned by the Municipality and submitted partial report on the results of the count, Also, property cards were already purchased during the year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 5 Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the:

a) Municipal Treasurer continuously pursue extensive collection of water service fees from delinquent water subscribers to augment the income of the Municipality and being the concerned official by devising strategies to make these subscribers aware on the regulations of the Municipality particularly in payment of their obligations; and

b) Municipal Administrator must ensure that the present water pumps were in good operating conditions to be able to provide adequate water supply for the subscribers and see to it that all subscribers are installed with water meters; to take immediate actions on requests for repairs, conduct periodic inspection and calibration of water meters ad replace defective ones to ensure the reliability and accuracy of consumption of every household.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to lessen the uncollectible water service fees in CY 2020. The Municipal Administrator’s Office –Waterworks Operations continuously maintains operational water pumps so subscribers would have sufficient water supply. As to water meters, it is included in the procurement plan of the LGU to purchase good quality water meters to be provided at reasonable price pursuant to existing relative ordinance. Defective ones could be replaced which in effect would result to reliable and accurate reading of consumed water of each household/establishment. Requests for repair are immediately responded once reported to the section/person-in-charge. Moreover, with the continuing increase of subscribers, the municipality takes action by constructing additional water reservoir on different identified sources to meet the demand and deliver safe/potable water to the people.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 15 Part II of this report.

We recommended the Management to refrain from paying travelling expenses/allowances and per diem to individuals hired through job order contract. Moreover, Management should stop the practice of allowing JO personnel to attend trainings/conferences/seminars as such opportunities should be given to regular employees. This practice indicates the presence of an employer-employee relationship, or may be considered as such, which runs counter to the above cited provisions.

direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budget for the payment of registration fees of the subject vehicles with LTO.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Assessor immediately effect the general revision of real property assessments, and to observe strict implementation of the provisions of Section 219 of RA 7160 considering that collection from RPT is one of the major sources of local taxes.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the:
Municipal Treasurer continuously pursue extensive collection of water service fees from delinquent water subscribers to augment the income of the Municipality and being the concerned official by devising strategies to make these subscribers aware on the regulations of the Municipality particularly in payment of their obligations; and
Municipal Administrator must ensure that the present water pumps were in good operating conditions to be able to provide adequate water supply for the subscribers and see to it that all subscribers are installed with water meters; to take immediate actions on requests for repairs, conduct periodic inspection and calibration of water meters ad replace defective ones to ensure the reliability and accuracy of consumption of every household.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Management to:
direct the drivers of the service vehicles to properly accomplish the Daily Trip Tickets and attach to the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels regularly;
require the Municipal Administrator to install control measures on the disbursements of fuel to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption and ensure strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 for the proper accounting and control of fuel consumption of government vehicles and other machineries and equipment of the Municipality; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure proper processing of transactions by religiously requiring complete documentation before payment.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive, through the Municipal Accountant, (i) require the persons liable to show proof that their suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishment and other personal efforts resulted in monetary savings to the Municipal Government, otherwise, require to refund the amount received by them; and (ii) henceforth, refrain from granting the monetary incentives without legal basis.

We recommended the Management to:
direct the Human Resource Management Officer to ensure the adherence of municipal employees with regards to accomplishing DTRs and Applications for Leave and to submit a justification/explanation for repeatedly ignoring this requirement;
require the municipal employees to attach the corresponding DTRs, Approved Application for Leave and other pertinent supporting documents as required by the nature of expense for the payment of salaries and other claims; and
instruct the Accounting Office to revisit the existing disbursement controls of the Municipality and install appropriate measures designed to ensure that no claim would be paid unless supported by valid attachments.

We recommended the Management to refrain from paying travelling expenses/allowances and per diem to individuals hired through job order contract. Moreover, Management should stop the practice of allowing JO personnel to attend trainings/conferences/seminars as such opportunities should be given to regular employees.

We recommended the Management to:
(a) direct the concerned municipal officials to comply with the prohibitions set forth under Civil Service Commission Resolution No. 020790 dated June 5, 2002;
(b) instruct the Human Resource and Management Officer to:
(i) evaluate the current regular personnel plantilla to determine if there are existing positions similar to the designations given to JOs ; and
(ii) in case none, make representation with the higher management to create positions to help augment the current workforce of those departments in need; and
(c) facilitate the recruitment and filling-up of vacant positions found in the present regular plantilla to avoid hiring JOs who had reached the compulsory retirement age.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and the Management agreed to:
develop a well-defined policy and guidelines in the granting of assistance to individuals and make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to pass and enact a resolution approving the said policies and guidelines; and
require the Municipal Accountant to oversee and ensure that every disposition of government funds is duly supported by documents in accordance with Section 4 of PD 1445 and that applicable laws, rules and regulations are observed in the disbursement.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the concerned accountable officers maintain a cashbook to record their collections and remittances in compliance with Section 63 of LTOM. The AOs should record their daily collections based on the official receipts issued (Debit Column) and remittances (Credit Column) to the designated Liquidating Officer or Municipal Treasurer. At the end of each month, AOs shall rule and foot the cashbook and show the ending balance of their accountabilities. Further, the AOs shall certify on the correctness and completeness of the entries in the cashbook.

We recommended and agreed Management to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
monitor the expiration dates of the fidelity bonds and ensure that the renewal of the bonds are made prior to its expiration; and
refrain from utilizing job order and non-bonded personnel in undertaking collecting functions for the Municipality and designate only those regular personnel who are properly bonded as collecting officers.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
prioritize the appointment of these permanent positions by hiring qualified personnel and expedite the promotion of deserving employees to comply with the provisions of RA No. 7160 as well as to provide equal job opportunities to the community;
require the HRMO to publish  the Notice of Vacancy and to fast track the selection of qualified applicants for the 17 vacant plantilla positions and the subsequent appointment thereof; and
consider to evaluate the performance of individuals under Job Order Contracts to open opportunity for possible appointment to permanent/regular positions.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant be directed to submit the required monthly and quarterly financial reports not later than the twentieth day after the end of the month or quarter supported by a schedule of subsidiary ledger balances of the controlling accounts in the General Ledger and the year-end financial statements not later than the fourteenth day of February after the end of the calendar year.

We recommended the Management to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and effect necessary adjustment to record the audit disallowances totaling ₱9,600.00 which become final and executory; (b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i) take the necessary actions to expedite the collection of the unsettled disallowances; (ii) exhaust all possible remedies to collect from each of the liable persons; (c) subsequently, cause all the certifying and approving officers, who took part in the disbursement of disallowed expenditures, to assume responsibility in the final settlement of the remaining balance of the disallowance; and (d) ensure that before allowing settlement of disallowances through installment payment the same was authorized and approved by COA.

We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to (a) compute the correct depreciation expense of the PPEs taking into consideration the factors in determining the useful life of an asset as provided in Paragraph 72 of IPSAS No. 17 and record immediately in the books of accounts in order to fairly present the carrying value of PPEs in the financial statements; (b) prepare necessary journal entries to record adjustments in the government equity account for prior year’s unrecorded depreciation; (c) submit list of PPEs transferred from CIP to specific asset accounts duly supported with the computation of the corresponding depreciation; and (d) exert effort in the segregation of PPE accounts previously in a lumped amount.

instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate fund for titling of the subject parcels of land.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the Management to:
instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) reclassify the granting of financial assistance to various non-governmental organizations/people’s organizations from Donation and/or Training Expense to Due from NGOs/POs; (ii) prepare the appropriate adjusting entries as Prior Year’s Adjustments to reclassify the expense account and to establish receivable account for those donations granted to NGOs/POs; and (iii) demand submission of the liquidation reports/accomplishment reports from recipient NGOs/POs as basis for closing the receivable account;
submit Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the NGOs/POs to support the payments; and
stop granting subsidy to NGOs/POs that do not clearly define the projects as enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.

We recommended that the Management:
require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting for inventories as provided under the NGAS Manual and proper inventory classification as prescribed by PPSAS No. 12;
designate an employee to act as Supply/Property Officer who will maintain stock cards and record the receipt and issuance of property and/or supplies and materials and prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued supported by Requisition and Issue Slip to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant to support recording of expenses in the books of accounts and adjusting the concerned inventory account; and
provide a stockroom for the inventory items of the agency to protect them from spillage/spoilage and unauthorized use.

We recommended that the Management:
require the Municipal Assessor to (i) take the initiative to conduct a timely physical survey in order to assess all real properties within the municipality’s territorial jurisdiction; and (ii) prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an updated assessment roll containing a list of all person whose real property have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties;
instruct the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis in recording the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables; and
require the Municipal Accountant to establish Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables based on actual receivables from said taxpayers.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the:
Municipal Treasurer pursue extensive collection of water service fee from delinquent water subscribers to augment the income of the Municipality and being the concerned official, must take appropriate actions on the matter;
Municipal Administrator must ensure that the present water pumps were in good operating conditions to be able to provide adequate water supply for the subscribers and see to it that all subscribers are installed with water meters; and
replace defective water meters and conduct periodic inspection and calibration to ensure their reliability and accuracy to determine accurate consumption of every household.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
direct the drivers of the service vehicles to properly accomplish the Daily Trip Tickets and attach to the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels regularly;
require the Municipal Administrator to install control measures on the disbursements of fuel to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption and ensure strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 for the proper accounting and control of fuel consumption of government vehicles and other machineries and equipment of the Municipality; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure proper processing of the payment by religiously requiring complete documentation before payment.

We recommended that the LCE, through the Municipal Accountant, (i) require the persons liable to show proof that their suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishment and other personal efforts resulted in monetary savings to the Municipal Government, otherwise, require to refund the amount received by them; and (ii) henceforth, refrain from granting the monetary incentives without legal basis.

In view of the above, we recommended that the Management:
develop a well-defined policy and guidelines in the granting of assistance to individuals and make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to pass and enact a resolution approving the said policies and guidelines; and
require the Municipal Accountant to oversee and ensure that every disposition of government funds is duly supported by documents in accordance with Section 4 of PD 1445 and that applicable laws, rules and regulations are observed in the disbursement.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed to:
instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) strictly observe/follow the updated description of accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009; (ii) reclassify the expenses to proper inventory or expense account; and (iii) furnish copy of Chart of Accounts to all offices/end-users for proper identification of accounts in the PPMP to be submitted to the Municipal Budget Office.
instruct the ICO-Municipal Budget Officer to adopt the new account codes as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 in the preparation of the agency’s budget.

We recommended the Management to:
direct the BAC as well as the Technical Working Group to strengthen control over the evaluation of bids and post-qualification of bidders to ensure completeness and veracity of the post-qualified documents. Moreover, observe strict adherence with the provisions of RA No. 9184 is enjoined with regard to all the procurement activities of the Municipality; and
require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the unexpended amount of P209,747.84 to the Bureau of Treasury.
require the Municipal Engineer and all officers designated as focal units, to strictly comply with the policy, guidelines and procedures in future receipt of aids from other national/local agencies.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the OIC - Municipal Budget Officer (OIC-MBO) and the Municipal Project Development Coordinator (MPDC) ensure the inclusion and re-appropriation of the savings of P2,939,205.45 in the preparation of the ensuing year’s Annual Investment Plan for 20% DF to optimize the fund and help augment the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcome of the municipality.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the Management to require the MDRRMO to optimally use the 70% Mitigation Fund through implementation of the planned programs/projects/activities in order to build a disaster resilient community, reduce disaster risks within the community and improve the capacity of the Municipality to respond to imminent dangers and emergencies.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:
require the Deputized MDDRMO to submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF duly certified by the Municipal Accountant, through LDRRMC and LDC, to the COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to facilitate the timely monitoring of implementation of LDRRMPs and balance of fund, if there is any, by the concerned offices, copy furnished Office of the Civil Defense and the Local Government Operation Officer of the Department of Interior and Local Government.
require the Secretariat of the MDRRMC to post the Report and Utilization of LDRRMF in the agency website, if any and in conspicuous places within the Municipality and other areas where the public always convene, for their information.

With the foregoing, we recommended the Management to require the MPDO and the members of GAD Focal Point System Council to:
institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning and policy formulation;
undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing as well as emerging gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and
revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.

We recommended the Management to:
direct the GAD focal person to conduct gender analysis of the agency’s programs and projects in the GPB to determine if the PPAs indicated therein addresses genuine local gender issues; and institute effective measures to monitor and ensure that PPAs are aligned to GAD-related undertakings.
observe strict adherence to the procedures and guidelines set forth in the IRR of RA 9710 in order to attain/achieve the objective of the “Magna Carta of Women”.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the:
Local School Board strictly utilize the SEF in compliance with the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.1 series of 2017 to effectively attain the intended purpose for which the fund was created; and
Municipal Government should refrain from allowing the SEF fund to be utilized for expenses not authorized in the aforementioned law and regulations.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed to:
prioritize the appointment of the mandatory position of Municipal Budget Officer by hiring qualified personnel and expedite the promotion of deserving employees to comply with the provisions of RA No. 7160 as well as to provide equal job opportunities to the community; and
require the HRMO to publish the Notice of Vacancy and to fast track the selection of qualified applicants for the 16 vacant plantilla positions and the subsequent appointment thereof.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Assessor immediately effect the general revision of real property assessments, and to observe strict implementation of the provisions of Section 219 of RA 7160 considering that collection from RPT is one of the major sources of local taxes.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to examine thoroughly all disbursement vouchers as to necessity, validity and completeness of the supporting documents to avoid excessive and unnecessary claims and payment of expenditures without proper and complete documentations.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to:
require the Municipal Treasurer to (i) exert extra effort to be able to produce the documents to prove ownership of the subject vehicles; (ii) coordinate with the PCSO for the transfer of ownership of Mitsubishi L300 Van with plate no. SHS 660 to the Municipality; (iii) secure Deed of Conveyance and/or Affidavit from Mr. Jonathan M. Garcia, owner of Suzuki Multicab with plate number XTC 779,  transferring all his rights and privileges over the subject vehicle to the Municipality of Mogpog to facilitate its registration; (iv) ensure the yearly renewal of registration of motor vehicles already registered under the name of the Municipality; and (v) coordinate with LTO for the immediate registration of the subject motor vehicles; and
direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budget for the payment of registration fees of the subject vehicles with LTO.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to submit the required monthly and quarterly financial reports not later than the twentieth day after the end of the month or quarter supported by a schedule of subsidiary ledger balances of the controlling accounts in the General Ledger.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed to:
require the Disbursing Officer to establish and maintain the required Cashbook for Disbursing Officer to commence from the last cash examination conducted, close the cashbook at the end of each month by double-ruling  the last entries;
ensure to keep an updated cashbook which shall be made available to management and COA in the conduct of audit of cash and accounts of the accountable officer; and
ensure the accuracy of the cash on hand by reconciling daily with the cash balance per cashbook.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer close the depository account with the Philippine National Bank and transfer its balance to existing accounts maintained with the LBP in compliance with Section 311 of the Local Government Code and Department of Finance Department Circular No. 01-2017. Otherwise, the administrative sanctions provided under Section 6 of the afore-mentioned Department Circular will be implemented.

instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher and effect necessary adjustment to record the audit disallowances totaling ₱1,210,360.17 which become final and executory;
require the Municipal Accountant to submit written justification for failure to record the aforementioned disallowances which became final and executory; and
ensure adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 on Settlement of Accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to (a) require the (i) Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting for inventories as provided under the NGAS Manual and proper inventory classification as prescribed by PPSAS No. 12; (ii) Supply/Property Officer should maintain stock cards to determine reorder point and prepare the necessary report on materials issued to support recording of expenses in the books of accounts, then submit to the Municipal Accountant the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued supported by Requisition and Issue Slip to adjust the concerned inventory account; and (b) provide a stockroom for the inventory items of the agency to protect them from spillage/spoilage and unauthorized use.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:

Municipal Accountant (i) establish Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables based on actual receivables from said taxpayers, and (ii) effect necessary adjustment to these receivable accounts to reflect actual balances of RPT and SET Receivables.

Municipal Treasurer pursue extensive collection of water service fee from delinquent water subscribers to augment the income of the Municipality and being the concerned official, must take appropriate actions on the matter;
Municipal Administrator must ensure that the present water pumps were in good operating conditions to be able to provide adequate water supply for the subscribers and see to it that all subscribers are installed with water meters; and
once installed, require periodic inspection and calibration to ensure their reliability and accuracy to determine accurate consumption of every household.
