seek refund of P9,600.00 from the concerned Administrative Support Staff of PLEB.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
Drivers of the service vehicles properly accomplish the Daily Trip Tickets and attached to the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travel regularly;
Municipal Administrator install control measures on the disbursements of fuel to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption and ensure strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 for the proper accounting and control of fuel consumption of government vehicles and other machineries and equipment of the Municipality; and
Municipal Accountant ensure that all transactions are supported with complete documentation before payment.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management:
ensure that each PPAs in the AIP are with specific allocations that will enable monitoring and evaluation of implementation of the Municipality’s development projects;
adhere strictly to the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to ensure efficient output, come up with projects with developmental impact that would improve more the socio-economic condition of the greater number of its constituents; and
identify the programs and projects that must be prioritized for inclusion and consideration in the Annual Investment Plan.  For those which are inconsistent with the governing rules and regulations, initiate amendments or realignment of the latter to those that address the needs of the constituents as far as tangible projects and programs are concerned.

We recommended that the LDRRMO fully and efficiently implement the planned programs/activities and prioritize projects included in their LDRRM Plan specifically 70% of the Fund to support disaster risk management activities such as disaster preparedness programs, procurement of life saving rescue tools and equipments and establishment of evacuation centers so that the utilization of the DRRM funds would be maximized and the intended benefits would be achieved.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Assessor effect general revision of real property assessments, and to observe strict implementation of the provisions of Section 219 of RA 7160 considering that collection from RPT is one of the major sources of local taxes.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to submit the required monthly, quarterly and year-end financial reports on time as prescribed under Section 70 and 73 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I or as may be provided by the New Government Accounting Manual (GAM). In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe/follow updated description of accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and reclassify the expenses to proper inventory or expense account.

Municipal Treasurer coordinate with LTO for the immediate registration of the motor vehicles; and
Municipal Budget Officer provide budget for the payment of registration fees of the subject vehicles with LTO.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:
discourage the use of reimbursement basis in payment of goods and services;
enforce strictly that all payments for the procurement of goods and services be made by check directly to suppliers/dealers/creditors to whom payments are due; and
deduct the applicable withholding taxes for every purchase.

require the Accountable Officer to establish and maintain the required Cashbook for Disbursing Officer to commence from the last cash examination conducted, close the cashbook at the end of each month by double-ruling  the last entries;

follow-up the compliance with the recommendations  for re-submission  to the national council for the latter’s review and approval and thereby implementation by the agency; and
submit immediately to the Provincial Committee on Solid Waste Management for their consolidation to come up with a Consolidated 10-year Solid Waste Development Program.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Assessor effect general revision of real property assessments, and to observe strict implementation of the provisions of Section 219 of RA 7160 considering that collection from RPT is one of the major sources of local taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We reiterated that the:
Municipal Treasurer (i) exert extra effort to be able to produce the documents to prove ownership of these vehicles; and (ii) secure the original OR/CR of the subject vehicles with green plates together with Deed of Conveyance and/or Affidavit from Mr. Jonathan Garcia, former Mayor of Mogpog and owner of Suzuki Multicab with plate number XTC 779,  transferring all his rights and privileges over the subject vehicle to the Municipality of Mogpog to facilitate its registration;
Municipal Treasurer coordinate with LTO for the immediate registration of the motor vehicles; and
Municipal Budget Officer provide budget for the payment of registration fees of the subject vehicles with LTO.

In view of the above observations, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the abovementioned required documents relative to the purchase of land by the Municipality to avoid suspension in audit. Moreover, require strict adherence to the guidelines set forth in Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 known as the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have  submitted the required documents relative to the purchase of land.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above, we recommended the Management to pursue extensive collection of water service fee from delinquent water subscribers to augment the income of the Municipality.  The Municipal Treasurer, being the concerned official, must take appropriate actions on the matter. In addition, the Municipal Administrator’s Office must see to it that all subscribers are installed with water meters.  Once installed, require periodic inspection and calibration to ensure their reliability and accuracy to determine accurate consumption of every household.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer commented that they have been prompt in submitting list of water service delinquency however due to poor water supply, her office could not fully collect water service fees from subscribers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to avoid incurrence of stale checks by closely monitoring the issuance and releases of checks; instruct the Municipal Accountant to analyze and adjust the balance of Cash in Bank account for the amount of stale checks to present fairly the account in the financial statements; and observe the procedure set forth in Section 59 Volume 1 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have analyzed reconciling items and adjusted the balance of Cash in Bank accounts for the amount of stale checks.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipality’s Bids and Awards Committee, in all stages of the procurement process,  be advised  to abide by the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 especially Section 13 thereof  in the conduct of competitive public bidding.
observations.managementAction: The BAC had already complied with Section 13 of IRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation:
In this regard, we recommended the following:

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer or the Supply/Administrative Officer to see to it that in the procurement of goods, the corresponding Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) conforms with the PBDs;

Direct the above Officer, the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to facilitate the preparation of the ABC for every procurement of infrastructure activity in accordance with the provisions of the foregoing department order and its attachment; and

Ensure that the subject document embodies the realistic quantities and costs based on the existing rates and estimates in order for the local government unit to conform with the guidelines set forth on this matter.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and MEO informed the Audit Team that they have facilitated the preparation of the Approved Budget of the Contract (ABC) in infrastructure in accordance with the provisions od PBDs.
The BAC Secretariat during the exit conference informed the Audit Team that  they will submit the required report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to require the supplier to pay the liquidated damages in the amount of ₱68,000.00 for its failure to deliver the subject rescue vehicle within the specified delivery schedule in accordance with Item 3 Annex D of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team that liquidated damages was not imposed to the dealer/supplier.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above, we recommended and Management agreed to the following:

Require the drivers of the service vehicles to properly accomplish the Daily Trip Tickets and ensure the same is approved by the authorized officials and signed by the end users/passengers of the motor vehicle and must be serially numbered.

Ensure that all disbursements of fuel are supported with complete documentation.  

Install control measures on the disbursements of fuel  to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption and ensure strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 for the proper accounting and control of fuel consumption of government vehicles and other machineries and equipment of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team that the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Reports of Travel were submitted however the corresponding driver’s tickets were still not attached.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 7 of this report

observations.recommendation: In view of the above, we recommended the Management to require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office and the Office of the Senior Citizens Affairs to intensify their coordination with regards to assessment, verification and monitoring of eligible indigent senior citizens; require the designated Disbursing Officer to use the updated and approved list of verified beneficiaries as basis for the preparation of payroll and payment thereof.
observations.managementAction: The MSWD is already compliant with said observation.

observations.recommendation: In view of above-mentioned observation, we recommended the Management to require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the fidelity bond of every accountable officer for public funds and request for an increase of bond since the accountability exceeds ₱750,000.00; and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that no cash advances will be granted in excess of the maximum accountability allowed in the bond.
observations.managementAction: The MTO have increased the amount of bond to cover the increase of accountability

observations.recommendation: The above transaction may be disallowed in audit, we recommended to the Management to observe strict compliance of the provisions of the DBM Budget Circular No. 2014-2 dated December 2, 2014 prescribing the guidelines on the grant of Collective Negotiation Agreement Incentive for 2014.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is compliant of the provisions of DBM Budget Circular 2014-2 prescribing the guidelines on the grant of Collective Negotiation Agreement.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above observation, we recommended the Management to strictly adhere to the law on the granting of additional benefits to its officials and employees particularly the Productivity Enhancement Incentive (PEI) which is uniformly given to government employees every year. Likewise, if found that the grant is not compliant with the PS limitation pursuant to Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160, the excess payment should be refunded immediately by all the recipients.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adhered to the law om granting of additional benefits of its officials and employees for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO be required to submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The Management failed to religiously  submit the required report.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we recommended the Management to:
Observe strict adherence to the guidelines for the preparation of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and corresponding Accomplishment Report and for the submission of copies thereof to the Auditor as provided in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; and 

Ensure that the Annual GAD Plan and Budget was approved by the DILG before incorporating to the Annual Budget so that the programs that will be implemented under GAD are directed to improving the municipal government’s capacity for gender mainstreaming, gender issues and/or promoting gender equality;

The non-submission of the required accomplishment report precluded the Audit Team to conduct early review and evaluation of GAD programs and activities implemented by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person informed the Audit Team that the  GAD Plan and Budget have already been submitted to the DILG for review/observation. And still in the process of effecting the reviews/ observations of the latter.
